2604.05930.title: "I See What You Did There": Can Large Vision-Language Models Understand Multimodal Puns?
2604.05930.paper_summary: Puns are a common form of rhetorical wordplay that exploits polysemy and phonetic similarity to create humor. In multimodal puns, visual and textual elements synergize to ground the literal sense and evoke the figurative meaning simultaneously. Although Vision-Language Models (VLMs) are widely used in multimodal understanding and generation, their ability to understand puns has not been systematically studied due to a scarcity of rigorous benchmarks. To address this, we first propose a multimodal pun generation pipeline. We then introduce MultiPun, a dataset comprising diverse types of puns alongside adversarial non-pun distractors. Our evaluation reveals that most models struggle to distinguish genuine puns from these distractors. Moreover, we propose both prompt-level and model-level strategies to enhance pun comprehension, with an average improvement of 16.5% in F1 scores. Our findings provide valuable insights for developing future VLMs that master the subtleties of human-like humor via cross-modal reasoning.
2604.05930.paper_summary_zh: 雙關語是一種常見的修辭性文字遊戲，利用多義性和語音相似性來創造幽默。在多模態雙關語中，視覺和文本元素協同作用，以同時確立字面意義並喚起比喻意義。儘管視覺-語言模型（VLMs）在多模態理解和生成中被廣泛使用，但由於缺乏嚴謹的基準測試，它們理解雙關語的能力尚未得到系統性研究。為了解決這個問題，我們首先提出了一個多模態雙關語生成管道。然後，我們介紹了MultiPun，一個包含各種雙關語類型及對抗性非雙關語干擾項的數據集。我們的評估顯示，大多數模型在區分真正的雙關語和這些干擾項方面存在困難。此外，我們提出了促使層級和模型層級的策略來增強雙關語理解，F1分數平均提高了16.5%。我們的研究結果為開發未來能夠通過跨模態推理掌握人類幽默細微差別的VLMs提供了寶貴的見解。
2604.05930.author: Naen Xu et.al.
2604.05930.authors: Naen Xu, Jiayi Sheng, Changjiang Li, Chunyi Zhou, Yuyuan Li, Tianyu Du, Jun Wang, Zhihui Fu, Jinbao Li, Shouling Ji
